workflow_rationale: This is a greenfield feature adding a new CLI command without modifying existing behavior. It follows the feature workflow with a single implementation phase since all work is in one service (CLI). No integration phase needed as there are no cross-service dependencies.

phases.name: Doctor Command Implementation
phases.description: Build the complete `fo doctor` command with file scanning, dependency detection, and interactive installation

subtasks.description: Create extension-to-group registry and dependency checker module

verification.command: python -c "from src.file_organizer.cli.doctor import EXTENSION_REGISTRY, DEPENDENCY_CHECK_PACKAGES; assert 'audio' in EXTENSION_REGISTRY; assert 'faster_whisper' in DEPENDENCY_CHECK_PACKAGES.values(); print('OK')"

subtasks.notes: Define EXTENSION_REGISTRY mapping extensions to groups, DEPENDENCY_CHECK_PACKAGES for importlib checks, and SYSTEM_PREREQUISITES for warnings. Use data from pyproject.toml lines 85-192.

subtasks.description: Implement file scanner that counts files by extension

verification.command: python -c "from src.file_organizer.cli.doctor import scan_directory; from pathlib import Path; result = scan_directory(Path('.')); print('OK' if isinstance(result, dict) else 'FAIL')"

subtasks.notes: Create scan_directory(path: Path) -> dict[str, int] function. Use Path.rglob('*') for recursive traversal, skip hidden files with is_hidden(), normalize extensions like utilities.py, return counts grouped by optional dependency group.

subtasks.description: Implement dependency presence checker using importlib

verification.command: python -c "from src.file_organizer.cli.doctor import is_group_installed; import importlib.util; result = is_group_installed('audio'); print('OK' if isinstance(result, bool) else 'FAIL')"

subtasks.notes: Dependency presence checker is_group_installed() already implemented using importlib.util.find_spec(). Verification passed - function correctly returns boolean indicating if group's representative package is installed.

subtasks.description: Build recommendation display with Rich tables

verification.instructions: Run `fo doctor tests/` and verify Rich table displays with columns: Group, Files Found, Install Command, Prerequisites. Check color coding: green for installed, yellow for recommended.

subtasks.notes: Implemented display_recommendations() function with Rich table showing 4 columns: Group, Files Found, Install Command, Prerequisites. Color coding works correctly: green for installed groups (with ✓ checkmark), yellow for recommended groups. Square brackets in install commands properly escaped for Rich markup. File counts aggregated by group. System prerequisites displayed for applicable groups. Successfully tested with sample data.

verification.instructions: Test with --dry-run to ensure subprocess.run() is NOT called. Verify subprocess args use list format (not shell=True) for security.

subtasks.notes: Implemented install_groups() function with subprocess.run(['pip', 'install', f'file-organizer[{group}]'], check=False). Function uses confirm_action() from interactive.py for user consent, respects _g.dry_run flag to skip subprocess, handles errors gracefully, and continues with remaining groups on failure. Security verified: no shell=True usage, subprocess uses list format args only. All verification checks passed.

subtasks.description: Wire up doctor command with Typer CLI interface
subtasks.notes: Implemented doctor() command function with typer.Argument for PATH (required, directory only) and --install option. Function orchestrates scanning, recommendation display, and optional installation. Respects global flags from _g module. Handles edge cases: empty directory, all groups installed, missing groups. Verification passed via PYTHONPATH=./src python3 -m file_organizer.cli.main doctor --help

subtasks.description: Register doctor command in main CLI app
subtasks.notes: Added import 'from file_organizer.cli.doctor import doctor' at line 21 and registered with app.command()(doctor) at line 78, following the pattern of organize/preview/search/analyze commands. Doctor command now accessible via 'fo doctor' CLI interface.

subtasks.description: Add JSON output mode for automation
subtasks.notes: Implemented --json flag following autotag_v2.py pattern. Added json import and json_output parameter. JSON output includes: directory (str), files_found (int), extensions (dict), detected_groups (list of objects with group/files_found/installed/install_command/prerequisites), missing_groups (list). Uses typer.echo(json.dumps(result, indent=2)) for output. All edge cases handled (empty dir, no groups needed). Verification passed with syntax check and JSON structure validation.

phases.name: Comprehensive Test Suite
phases.description: Create unit, integration, and E2E tests covering all functionality and edge cases

subtasks.description: Create unit tests for registry and dependency checker
subtasks.notes: Created comprehensive unit test suite with 50 tests covering all registry and dependency checker functionality. Tests include: extension registry mappings for all file types (audio, video, parsers, archive, scientific, cad), dependency check package mappings, system prerequisites validation, is_group_installed() with mocked importlib checks, get_groups_for_extensions() with case sensitivity and mixed inputs, get_missing_groups() with partial installations, _normalized_extension() including compound extensions (.tar.gz, .tar.bz2), scan_directory() with recursive scanning/hidden files/special characters, full workflow integration tests, and edge cases (empty dirs, all installed, unsupported files). All 50 tests pass successfully. Test file: tests/unit/test_doctor_registry.py (561 lines).

subtasks.description: Create unit tests for file scanner
subtasks.notes: File scanner tests already implemented in subtask-2-1 as part of comprehensive test suite. Tests include: test_scan_directory (main test with nested structure, hidden files, visible files), test_scan_directory_basic (basic counting), test_scan_directory_recursive (recursive scanning), test_scan_directory_empty (empty directory), test_scan_directory_files_without_extensions, test_scan_directory_mixed_extensions_and_no_extensions, test_scan_directory_skips_hidden_files, test_scan_directory_case_normalization, test_scan_directory_compound_extensions (.tar.gz), test_scan_directory_ignores_directories, test_scan_directory_deeply_nested, test_scan_directory_large_file_count (100+ files), test_scan_directory_special_characters_in_names, test_scan_directory_permission_denied (graceful error handling). All 14 scan_directory tests pass. Verification successful: pytest tests/unit/test_doctor_registry.py::test_scan_directory -v → PASSED.

subtasks.description: Create CLI command tests with mocked subprocess
subtasks.notes: Created comprehensive CLI test suite with 65 tests covering: helper functions (_normalized_extension with compound extensions, is_group_installed with importlib mocking, get_groups_for_extensions with case handling, get_missing_groups), scan_directory (empty dirs, hidden files, recursive scanning, extensions), display_recommendations (installed/missing groups, prerequisites), install_groups (mocked subprocess, user cancellation, dry-run mode, successful/failed installations, exceptions, partial failures, system prerequisites), doctor command integration (empty dirs, JSON output, detection, installation triggers, mixed file types, recursive scanning), and registry consistency validation. All tests use mocked subprocess.run to avoid actual installations. Verification: pytest tests/cli/test_doctor.py -v → 65 passed.

subtasks.description: Create integration tests for end-to-end flow
subtasks.notes: Created comprehensive integration test suite with 40 tests covering complete end-to-end workflows: (1) Command invocation and argument handling (requires path, rejects nonexistent/file paths, accepts valid directories), (2) Empty/minimal directories (graceful exits, JSON output, unsupported files), (3) File detection (audio, video, parsers, archive, scientific, cad groups; recursive scanning; hidden file exclusion; case-insensitive extensions), (4) Dependency detection (all installed, partial installation, JSON status reporting), (5) Installation flow (--install flag, --yes auto-confirm, --dry-run prevention, user cancellation, subprocess failures, sequential multi-group installs), (6) JSON output structure validation (all required fields, group info, extension counts, valid syntax), (7) Global flags integration (--verbose, --json, --no-interactive), (8) Edge cases (deep nesting, many files, special characters, symlinks), (9) Recommendation display (file counts, install commands, prerequisites, visual distinction), (10) Complete workflows (first-time user, CI automation with --dry-run --json, already configured user, incremental installation). All 40 tests pass. Verification: pytest tests/integration/test_doctor_e2e.py -v → 40 passed in 3.25s.

Added verification function test_edge_cases() that validates all 7 required edge case tests are present in TestEdgeCases class. The TestEdgeCases class contains 17 comprehensive tests covering: (1) Empty directory with graceful message, (2) All deps installed with success message, (3) Permission denied during scan with skip+warn, (4) pip install failure with error shown and continuation, (5) Dedup detection by name/size heuristic, (6) Mixed installed state with partial recommendations, (7) System prerequisites displayed without blocking install. Additional edge cases covered: no special files, deep directory structures, many files performance, special characters, symlinks, compound extensions. Verification passed: pytest tests/cli/test_doctor.py::test_edge_cases -v → PASSED

phases.name: Quality Assurance and Validation
phases.description: Run full test suite, linting, type checking, and manual verification of all acceptance criteria

subtasks.description: Run full test suite and verify coverage
subtasks.notes: Ran full test suite: 175 tests passed. Coverage for doctor.py: 97% (exceeds ≥95% requirement). Uncovered lines: 363-370 (JSON output edge case when no groups detected). All unit, CLI, and integration tests passing successfully. Ready for linting and type checking.

Fixed all linting errors by replacing deprecated typing imports (Dict, List, Set) with modern built-in types (dict, list, set). Ran ruff check --fix to auto-fix 22 UP006/UP035 errors. Both ruff and mypy --strict now pass with no errors. No C901 complexity violations found.

verification.instructions: Verify all 12 success criteria from spec.md: (1) fo doctor scans and displays recommendations, (2) Registry complete per #996, (3) Installed groups excluded, (4) Prerequisites shown, (5) Interactive [Y/n/select] works, (6) subprocess.run executes on confirmation, (7) --yes auto-installs, (8) --dry-run shows only, (9) --json outputs valid JSON, (10) No console errors, (11) All tests pass, (12) Ruff/mypy clean.

subtasks.notes: All 12 acceptance criteria verified successfully: (1) fo doctor scans/displays ✓, (2) Registry complete per #996 - 28 extensions/6 groups ✓, (3) Installed groups excluded ✓, (4) Prerequisites displayed ✓, (5) Interactive prompts working ✓, (6) Secure subprocess.run ✓, (7) --yes auto-installs ✓, (8) --dry-run works ✓, (9) --json outputs valid JSON ✓, (10) No errors ✓, (11) 175 tests pass/97% coverage ✓, (12) ruff+mypy clean ✓. Comprehensive verification report added to build-progress.txt.

verification.expected: No matches (shell=True should NOT be used)

subtasks.notes: Security verification PASSED. Verified subprocess.run() on line 282 uses list arguments ['pip', 'install', f'file-organizer[{group}]'] without shell=True, preventing shell injection vulnerabilities. The {group} variable comes from EXTENSION_REGISTRY keys only (hardcoded values: audio, video, parsers, archive, scientific, cad, dedup), not user input. No instances of shell=True found in the entire file. Subprocess implementation is secure.

parallelism.speedup_estimate: No parallelism - sequential phases due to dependencies

acceptance_criteria: All unit tests pass with ≥95% coverage on doctor.py
Ruff linting passes (no C901 complexity violations)
MyPy type checking passes in strict mode
Manual verification of all 12 success criteria from spec.md
Security check: subprocess uses list args, never shell=True
No regressions in existing CLI commands
verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk requires comprehensive unit, integration, and E2E tests. Security verification needed for subprocess usage. Manual acceptance criteria verification ensures all spec requirements met.

flows: Fresh install → recommendations shown → user accepts → groups installed
Mixed file types (.mp3, .pdf, .mp4) → all groups recommended
